title: Elatostema latifolium
taxonomy: Elatostema latifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the nettle family. This family consists of over 2,000 species of plants, many of which are native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. Elatostema latifolium is a member of the genus Elatostema, which includes approximately 200 species of plants. The Urticaceae family is part of the larger order Rosales, which also includes other families such as the rose and buckthorn families. Elatostema latifolium is native to Asia, where it can be found growing in shaded areas and along streams.
uses: Elatostema latifolium, a species native to the Himalayas, has been found to have potential medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Elatostema latifolium is used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems. intro: Elatostema latifolium is a perennial plant species in the family Urticaceae, commonly known as the 'Broad-leaved Polynesian Vines' or 'Pakalana'. The health benefits of Elatostema latifolium include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, wounds, and other infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Elatostema latifolium is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, skin irritations, and digestive issues.
